message:
fix(inode,core): widen ext4 timestamp pipeline to 64-bit seconds for year-2106+ support

encode_extra_timestamp now properly encodes epoch extension bits (secs >> 32)
into the _extra fields, supporting the full ext4 34-bit timestamp range through
~2446. All timestamp functions (touch_atime, touch_mtime_ctime, touch_ctime,
create_inode, delete_inode, now_timestamp) accept u64 seconds. Adds test
coverage for epoch extension encoding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-2s4.5","title":"E2E test: SIGKILL during write, remount, verify consistency","description":"# E2E test: SIGKILL during write, remount, verify consistency

## GOAL
Verify that FrankenFS maintains consistency guarantees after an unclean shutdown (SIGKILL during active writes). The filesystem must be mountable, crash recovery must work, and all committed data must be intact.

## TEST SCENARIO

### Phase 1: Setup
1. Create fresh 256MB ext4 image
2. Mount via FUSE, write baseline dataset (500 files, checksums recorded)
3. fsync all files, verify baseline is durable
4. Begin continuous write workload (background thread writing new files)

### Phase 2: Crash
5. After 2 seconds of active writing, send SIGKILL to FUSE process
6. Record which files had fsync called vs which were in-flight

### Phase 3: Recovery
7. Remount the filesystem image
8. Crash recovery should trigger automatically (journal replay, MVCC rollback)
9. Record evidence ledger output

### Phase 4: Verification
10. All baseline files (pre-crash) must be intact with correct checksums
11. Files that had fsync completed must be intact
12. Files that were in-flight at SIGKILL time: acceptable to be missing or truncated, but:
    - Must NOT contain garbage data
    - Must NOT corrupt other files
    - Directory must be consistent (no dangling entries)
13. No filesystem-level corruption (fsck-equivalent check passes)

### Invariants
- Atomicity: a file write either fully committed or fully absent
- No cross-file corruption: crash during file A write must never affect file B
- Metadata consistency: directory entries match existing inodes
- Journal consistency: no partial journal transactions visible

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Filesystem mounts after SIGKILL without errors
- [ ] All pre-crash baseline files intact with correct checksums
- [ ] All fsync'd files intact
- [ ] In-flight files are either complete or cleanly absent (no partial garbage)
- [ ] No cross-file corruption
- [ ] Evidence ledger records crash recovery actions
- [ ] Test is reproducible (deterministic write pattern)
- [ ] Test completes in < 60s","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmGorge","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:40.893316017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:20:36.682290287Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:20:36.682269679Z","close_reason":"Completed: SIGKILL E2E now passes with deterministic crash invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","e2e","fuse","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:40.893316017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:10.730235864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:13.504998589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:08:10.911533057Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Resolved two blockers and validated end-to-end:

1) Clean unmount/remount durability blocker (from `bd-2s4.7`):
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`: RW mount now opens ext4 with `Ext4JournalReplayMode::Apply` (RO stays `SimulateOverlay`).

 {"id":"bd-2s4.6","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for FUSE dispatch and lifecycle

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the Production FUSE Runtime epic (bd-2s4). Currently the epic has E2E tests (bd-2s4.4, bd-2s4.5, bd-3en1) but NO dedicated unit tests for the dispatch layer, lifecycle management, or errno mapping.

## TEST PLAN

### 1. Dispatch Tests
- Thread pool spawns correct number of threads
- Concurrent requests are dispatched without deadlock
- Request timeout fires and returns EIO
- Queue depth metrics are updated correctly

### 2. Lifecycle Tests
- Mount initializes all subsystems in correct order
- Unmount flushes dirty data before teardown
- SIGTERM triggers clean unmount sequence
- Double-unmount is idempotent (no panic)
- Mount with invalid image returns descriptive error

### 3. Errno Mapping Tests
- Every FfsError variant maps to correct errno
- Unknown errors map to EIO
- Cancelled maps to EINTR
- CorruptData maps to EIO
- NoSpace maps to ENOSPC
- PermissionDenied maps to EACCES

### 4. State Tests
- FsOps state is Send + Sync (compile-time test)
- Concurrent getattr calls do not race
- Cache access under lock is bounded (no starvation)

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. All tests listed above
2. Total: ~20 unit tests
3. All deterministic, no I/O (mock BlockDevice)

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log full context (operation, expected, actual)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All dispatch, lifecycle, errno, and state tests pass
2. No flaky tests (deterministic)
3. Coverage of all FUSE op errno mappings","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:41.207992681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:01:59.609010121Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:01:59.608990174Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:41.207992681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:49.972174278Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","depends_on_id":"bd-cebm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.086927457Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2s4.7","title":"Fix ext4 RW write persistence across clean unmount/remount","description":"## Summary
In privileged E2E runs, ext4 RW operations succeed, but data written in phase 3 is missing after clean unmount + RO remount.

## Repro
1. Build `ffs-cli` (or use prebuilt binary).
2. Run:
   `sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" FFS_SKIP_BUILD=1 FFS_CLI_BIN=/data/projects/frankenfs/target/release/ffs-cli FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh`
3. Observe failure in phase 4: `persist.txt` is missing on RO remount.

## Evidence
- Run artifact: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_040650_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log`
- RO mount log: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_040650_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/mount_ro_mnt_ro.log`

## Expected
Writes acknowledged in RW mount (and fsync-backed sentinel writes) remain visible after clean unmount/remount.

## Actual
`persist.txt` is readable in RW mount, but absent after remount.

## Notes
- Prior RW EIO mount failure appears resolved in this environment.
- Unmount robustness was improved in `scripts/e2e/lib.sh`, so this now consistently surfaces a persistence/durability issue rather than mount teardown noise.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CalmGorge","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:07:52.623298157Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:40.625109148Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:40.625071358Z","close_reason":"Completed: fixed RW persistence loss by using Apply replay mode for RW mount","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","ext4","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:07:52.623298157Z","created_by":"CalmGorge","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":238,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resolved root cause in CLI mount path: `mount_cmd` was always opening ext4 with `Ext4JournalReplayMode::SimulateOverlay`, which made RW writes session-local and non-persistent.

Change:
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs`: added `ext4_mount_replay_mode(read_write)` and switched RW mount to `Apply` while keeping RO mount on `SimulateOverlay`.
- Added unit test `ext4_mount_replay_mode_is_persistent_for_rw`.

Validation:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-cli --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p ffs-cli --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p ffs-cli --all-targets` ✅
- Privileged E2E run: `sudo -n env PATH="$PATH" FFS_SKIP_BUILD=1 FFS_CLI_BIN=/data/projects/frankenfs/target/release/ffs-cli FFS_AUTO_UNMOUNT=0 ./scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh`
  - Phase 4 now passes: `persist.txt` and appended `mtime-check` survive clean unmount + RO remount.
  - Artifact: `artifacts/e2e/20260218_041747_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log`.","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:18:36Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2t1","title":"MVCC: Specify API + invariants (TxnId/CommitSeq/Snapshot) and phase plan (FCW -> SSI)","description":"Goal: make MVCC semantics unambiguous before integrating with filesystem operations.

Deliverables:
- Document: what a Snapshot means (visibility window), when it is taken, and how it is used for reads.
- Document: transaction lifecycle (begin -> stage writes -> commit/abort) and what is allowed concurrently.
- Phase plan:
  - Phase A: First-committer-wins (FCW) conflict check at commit.
  - Phase B: Add optional SSI tracking (read sets + rw-antidependencies) where needed.

Acceptance:
- ffs-mvcc public API is stable enough that ffs-fuse can build on it.
- There is a simple correctness proof sketch for FCW and a roadmap for SSI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:22:27.695269767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:36.304894733Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:20:36.304874716Z","close_reason":"Spec: clarify MVCC Snapshot/Transaction lifecycle; Phase A (FCW) vs Phase B (SSI) plan; document Phase A API","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mvcc"]}
 {"id":"bd-2tq","title":"Semantics: Define a minimal VFS operations trait for FUSE/harness","description":"Goal: define the internal interface FUSE and harness call.

Deliverables:
- A trait (e.g. FsOps) with methods needed for read-only mount:
  - getattr(inode)
  - lookup(parent,name)
  - readdir(inode)
  - read(inode,offset,len)
- Types for file attributes and directory entries.

Acceptance:
- ffs-fuse can implement the fuser::Filesystem methods by delegating to this trait.
- ext4 implementation can live behind this interface (and later btrfs too).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:26:14.391246994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:29:28.832170053Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:29:28.832148523Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["semantics"]}
 {"id":"bd-2tt","title":"Implement ext4 orphan inode recovery","description":"# Implement ext4 orphan inode recovery

## GOAL
Implement ext4 orphan inode list processing as required by FEATURE_PARITY.md: "ext4 orphan recovery parity: ❌ Not yet implemented"

## BACKGROUND
ext4 maintains an orphan inode list for crash recovery:
- Inodes being deleted but still open
- Truncated files with pending block release
- On mount, these must be cleaned up

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Orphan List Structure
```rust
/// ext4 orphan inode linked list
/// s_last_orphan in superblock -> first orphan inode
/// i_dtime in orphan inode -> next orphan (0 = end)
pub struct OrphanList {
    head: InodeNumber,
}

impl OrphanList {
    pub fn read(sb: &Ext4Superblock, device: &dyn BlockDevice) -> Result<Self>;
    pub fn iter(&self) -> OrphanIter;
}
```

### 2. Recovery Actions
```rust
pub fn recover_orphan(
    inode_num: InodeNumber,
    fs: &mut OpenFs,
    cx: &Cx,
) -> Result<OrphanAction> {
    let inode = fs.read_inode(inode_num)?;
    
    if inode.link_count == 0 {
        // Deleted file, reclaim blocks and inode
        reclaim_inode_blocks(inode_num, fs, cx)?;
        free_inode(inode_num, fs, cx)?;
        Ok(OrphanAction::Deleted)
    } else {
        // Truncated file, fix size
        truncate_to_size(inode_num, inode.size, fs, cx)?;
        Ok(OrphanAction::Truncated)
    }
}
```

### 3. Mount Integration
- Process orphan list before journal replay
- Clear orphan list after processing
- Log actions taken

### 4. Error Handling
- Invalid orphan inode -> log and skip
- Circular list -> detect and break
- I/O error -> mount read-only

## TESTS
1. Unit: Parse orphan list from superblock
2. Integration: Orphan with link_count=0 deleted
3. Integration: Orphan with link_count>0 truncated
4. Integration: Corrupt orphan list handled gracefully

## LOGGING
- Orphan list head (debug)
- Each orphan processed (info): inode, action
- Orphan list cleared (info)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All orphans processed on mount
2. Blocks correctly reclaimed
3. No orphan references remain
4. When complete: flip to ✅ in FEATURE_PARITY.md.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49Z"},{"id":54,"issue_id":"bd-2tt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REVIEW FIX: Added blocks deps on bd-huh.2 (block allocation) and bd-1xe.4 (journal replay write-back). Orphan recovery is a mutation operation that frees blocks and deletes inodes. It must happen AFTER journal replay (to operate on committed state) and requires the allocator (to reclaim blocks). The mount order is: (1) journal replay write-back, (2) orphan list reading, (3) orphan recovery.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:33:46Z"},{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-2tt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
